Intuition

chapter 1

I like to think I’ve always been intuitive. Seeing things other people can’t see. How the earth tells us things through unspoken words, the way the wind moves branches on trees, the way the waves crash together over and a over in a storm, the way grass stays green and continues to grow, the way the stars twinkle with such picturesque beauty, the way that stray cat let you pet it because you were kind to it, and, the way we take what the earth offers us for granted. I have always seen the unseen, always seek my unanswered questions, the things I don’t understand, within what the universe already renders. There are things in this world that defies what humanity has created, things people are not prepared for and things others have seen too.

I can always sense when things are about to change, pathetic fallacy if you will, the term for the attribution of conduct and human emotion found in nature or things not human. You may call me crazy, but I call it attention to detail, the knowing you get in the pit of your stomach when your life, or someone else’s is going to change for better or for worse. That knowing, the knowing, the awareness, of everything that breathes around me, everything that lives without oxygen, is what saved me from a fate I can’t remember anymore.

There are still many things I don’t have the answers to, but the world still whispers to me, accommodates me in my journey through life and in return, I praise the Earth like a God, treating it with the utmost respect and adoration, something a lot of humanity neglects to do anymore. Somewhere along the way, we lost respect for the very thing that provides for us permanently. Yet life goes on, as it tends to do, and it will continue to do so, it just hurts to watch everyone forgetting what and who it was made for.

There’s only one unanswered question of mine the universe hasn’t provided the answer for yet. Why can’t I remember the last time I woke up, the last time I brushed my hair, the last time I got ready to go out, the last time I walked into a room, and people, noticed me, the last time I… lived. Oh. I’m not really, here, am I? I guess it all makes sense now, why I’ve watched how things unfold for so long.

The universe finally gave me the answer to the burning question I’ve been wanting to know for what feels like forever, and as I feel myself fading away, the winds a little louder, the waves a little calmer, but before I go, that knowing hits me, my intuition serving me one last time, a new life is going to begin, therefore, my time of watching is over, I can fade away now, with all the answers I wanted to have. I will be one with the earth, I’ll be the branches blowing, the waves crashing and the grass growing. I like to think, I’ve always been intuitive.
